zh.lifeSummary: 當Elo Pérez de Castro於大約1196年出生在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納時，她的父親Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon37歲，母親Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo47歲。她於1215年與Guerau de Cabrera URGEL在西班牙結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Pérez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish (Pérez): patronymic from the personal name Pedro, an equivalent of Peter . This surname is also found in France. Compare De Perez .
Breton (Pérez): variant of Pérès (see Peres ).

variants.name: Parez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Pérez (see Perez ).
Czech (Pařez): nickname from pařez ‘(tree) stump’.
In some cases possibly also French (Nord), Walloon, and Flemish: variant of Paré (see Pare ) or Paret .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Jorge, Miguel, Francisco, Mario, Pedro, Raul.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Ligia, Vasco, Wenceslao, Armanda, Fernandes.

Galician, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): topographic name from castro ‘castle, fortress’ (from Latin castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word. See also De Castro .

Italian and Jewish (from Italy): topographic name for someone who lived by a fortification, from castro ‘fortification’ (see 1 above) or a habitational name for someone from the town of Castro, the center of a Duchy in Lazio (destroyed after its occupation by Papal military forces in 1649). Compare Lo Castro .

variants.name: Alcazar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Francisco, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Julio, Rafael, Raul, Ruben, Alicia, Gustavo.

Spanish (Alcázar): habitational name from any of various places, for example in the provinces of Ciudad Real, Cuenca, and Granada, named with the word alcázar ‘citadel’ or ‘palace’ (from Arabic al ‘the’ + qaṣr ‘fortress’, a borrowing of Latin castrum; compare Castro ).

variants.name: De Castro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Armando, Mario, Alfredo, Jorge, Julio, Manuel, Ricardo, Rolando, Alvaro.
Galician and Portuguese: habitational name for someone from any of various places called with castro ‘castle, fortress’ (see Castro ), with the preposition de ‘of, from’.

variants.name: Castrillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jorge, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Guillermo, Ramon, Alberto, Alejandro, Ana, Benito.
Spanish: habitational name from any of various places, especially in León, named Castrillo, from castrillo ‘fort’, a diminutive of Castro .

variants.name: Castronovo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Santo, Cosimo, Giacomo, Graziella, Sal, Stefano.
Italian (southern): habitational name from any of various places called Castronovo, from castro ‘fortification’ (see Castro ) + novo ‘new’.

nameMeanings.name: Elo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from a Hebrew word meaning ‘height’. This was borne by the priest and judge who brought up the future prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 4). It was especially popular among Puritans in the 17th century and has enjoyed a modest revival in recent years. As a Jewish name, it is also a short form of any of numerous names formed with el ‘God’, such as Eliezer , Elijah , and Elisha .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Elo Pérez de Castro was born about 1196, in Girona, Catalonia, Spain, her father, Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon, was 37 and her mother, Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo, was 47. She married Guerau de Cabrera URGEL in 1215, in Spain. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elo Pérez de Castro est née environ 1196, à Girona, Catalunya, Espagne, son père, Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo, avait 47 ans. Elle a épousé Guerau de Cabrera URGEL en 1215, à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille.

Sie hat 1215 in Spanien Guerau de Cabrera URGEL geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

ko.lifeSummary: Elo Pérez de Castro(이)가 대략 1196년 스페인 카털니아 지로나에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Pedro Fernandez "el Castellano" de Castro Lord of Paredes de Nava y Infantado de Léon(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jimena Gómez de Manzanedo(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 1215년 스페인에서 Guerau de Cabrera URGEL(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
